item.title: Version 1.1.1
item.description:
• Back/Forward 10% seek — Back-10% and Forward-10% buttons (and Shift + ←/→) are now available in every reading mode: Main-window inline reader, Separate-window panel, Notch widget, and the External Reader Bar.
  • Shift seek shortcut — the Shift + ←/→ keyboard shortcut works in Main, Notch, and Separate-window modes as well as Zen.
  • Consistent seeking math — all modes use the same percent-based seek behavior so skips are uniform across readers.
  • Zen chunked playback — for multi-word chunks (2+ words) the indicator now centers on the visual middle of the chunk for easier fixation; single-word mode remains unchanged.
  • Notch playback fix — pressing Play (or Space) during the Notch 3-2-1 countdown now cancels the countdown overlay and starts playback immediately so the first words are not hidden.

item.title: Version 1.1.0
item.description:
• Zen Mode — a new distraction-free reading experience that opens fullscreen on its own Space, with a centered word, optional upcoming-words context, drag-scrub progress, and the standard playback controls. Esc / ⌘W / Done to exit.
  • Top pause settings in Zen — tweak WPM, font size, words-at-a-time, upcoming words, auto-hide and loop right from the Zen window when paused, no need to leave fullscreen.
  • Words at a time — read 1–7 words per beat (classic chunked RSVP). Per-beat timing scales sub-linearly so chunked playback feels noticeably faster than single-word at the same WPM.
  • Loop and Auto-hide UI in Zen — auto-restart from the beginning when the article ends, and fade out the bottom controls after 2 s of inactivity.
  • Inline images — pictures from URL articles and DOCX documents now render in the preview and pause the reader on each image instead of being silently skipped.
  • External Reader Bar — when reading happens in Notch, Separate Window or Zen, the main window now shows a compact bar with progress, ±word, play/pause, Show, and Stop.
  • Reader holds on the current word for 0.6 s after any pause before resuming, giving your eye a moment to re-fixate.
  • Speed settings now stay synchronized between Settings and all reading modes.

item.title: Version 1.0.9
item.description:
• Reading markdown files flows much more naturally — code blocks, math formulas, images, tables, footnotes, and emoji shortcodes all render properly in the preview and read at a sensible pace, no more crawling through code one token at a time or sailing past pictures silently.
  • Markdown documents now have Preview / Edit tabs, matching the PDF flow — handy for skimming or copying the raw source. Nothing is written back to the file.
  • Settings → Contact got a refresh: dedicated Contact Support and Restore Purchases buttons that take you straight into the in-app customer portal where you can leave a request or sign in to recover your license.
  • Statistics → Avg Speed no longer reports impossible numbers — very short or accidental sessions are now excluded, and the average is bounded by your actual speed setting.
  • Several richer markdown features now render correctly: definition lists, abbreviations with hover tooltips, sub- and superscripts, marked / inserted text, container blocks, and typographic replacements like (c) → ©.

item.title: Version 1.0.8
item.description:
• Trial badge moved to the top of the content area — it stays visible regardless of sidebar state, and tapping it opens License settings directly.
  • Speed Reader can now be installed via Homebrew: brew install --cask speed-reader-pro/tap/speed-reader.

item.title: Version 1.0.7
item.description: App icon now renders correctly on all Macs — fixed a safe-area issue that made the icon look oversized with white corners on some displays.

item.title: Version 1.0.6
item.description: If you buy Speed Reader from the website while your free trial is running, the app now unlocks automatically — no need to wait for the trial to end or re-enter your email on the paywall.

item.title: Version 1.0.5
item.description:
• Your free trial is now tied to your email, so reinstalling the app or changing the system clock no longer resets it.
  • You can reopen the welcome guide anytime from Help without being asked for your email again.
  • The Settings window is a bit more compact.

item.title: Version 1.0.4
item.description: On a fresh install, the welcome guide now appears before the email prompt, and the email screen clearly mentions that you get 3 free days of full access.

item.title: Version 1.0.3
item.description: The app now reports the correct version number, which also stops the update prompt from reappearing after you've installed.

item.title: Version 1.0.2
item.description: The "Check for Updates" button and the automatic-update toggle in Settings now work properly.
